title: buildah pull docker.io/nvidia/cuda:9.2-cudnn7-devel fails with "Error committing the finished image"
body:
I cleaned up my images with:

    buildah rm -a
    buildah rmi -a

And deleted all my temporary shares left from previous half-built containers with

    rm -rf ~/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs/dir/*

Now, I try to download a previously successfully downloaded image with:

    buildah pull docker.io/nvidia/cuda:9.2-cudnn7-devel

However, the result is:

```
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:18d680d616571900d78ee1c8fff0310f2a2afe39c6ed0ba2651ff667af406c3e
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:0addb6fece630456e0ab187b0aa4304d0851ba60576e7f6f9042a97ee908a796
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:78e58219b215b359fe002f0ca1f416617b75ca9b36cb274c98d7a5f808711179
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:eb6959a66df2ea26a26452ba11f84fb64bb45af90204374862ec71ee59f795e7
Copying blob sha256:c7f8a5420911af2d94a05755e569e58da2e33de4b199fbc4a22771cd3f72023f
 6.65 MiB / 6.65 MiB [======================================================] 3s
Copying blob sha256:f20e55ec643a8ef56c529bdbc0ce5486c95735b52536330913d7c52003759e74
 386.20 KiB / 386.20 KiB [==================================================] 0s
Copying blob sha256:964bd7cf2ca319d92423a69eb3411a1480fd35287634cc6cc9edf6b2e53e85f9
 181 B / 181 B [============================================================] 0s
Copying blob sha256:db218380e512e0e48f2c3bf3fc9e9accb63778483193b4e41b6d98a5a3febff3
 466.85 MiB / 466.85 MiB [===============================================] 4m37s
Copying blob sha256:1a7ab6090777cfb12f1e111661f13d9cef97479b536e3646017913366b79bf96
 652.26 MiB / 652.26 MiB [================================================] 7m1s
Copying blob sha256:f846c9d928fd5f5ef3370df6e6f9068764566dabad8ba1e22770840bb9205d06
 358.83 MiB / 358.83 MiB [===============================================] 3m27s
Copying config sha256:7affc4c2ce6186d6caab0880673a2c29f909b4d120aabaa4c6b6b253d8176b64
 10.08 KiB / 10.08 KiB [====================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:c7f8a5420911af2d94a05755e569e58da2e33de4b199fbc4a22771cd3f72023f": error creating layer with ID "145e708298e47bd009de3d3788c903192fd9ba48b8a47cc768a25b75106254de": no such file or directory
ERRO[0922] exit status 1                                
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/xenial,now 1.5-1~dev~ubuntu16.04.2~ppa6 amd64 [installed,automatic]
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.5-dev
Go Version:      go1.10.4
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.7.0-alpha1
Git Commit:      18309de
Built:           Mon Nov 19 18:31:08 2018
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=16.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=xenial
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 16.04.5 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="16.04.5 LTS (Xenial Xerus)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 16.04.5 LTS"
VERSION_ID="16.04"
HOME_URL="http://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="http://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="http://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
VERSION_CODENAME=xenial
UBUNTU_CODENAME=xenial
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux xxx-P370SM-A 4.4.0-139-generic #165-Ubuntu SMP Wed Oct 24 10:58:50 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary read-write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional read-only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers (currently overlay, zfs, vfs, btrfs)
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body:
Note that `docker` works:

```
$ docker pull docker.io/nvidia/cuda:9.2-cudnn7-devel
9.2-cudnn7-devel: Pulling from nvidia/cuda
18d680d61657: Already exists 
0addb6fece63: Already exists 
78e58219b215: Already exists 
eb6959a66df2: Already exists 
c7f8a5420911: Pull complete 
f20e55ec643a: Pull complete 
964bd7cf2ca3: Pull complete 
db218380e512: Pull complete 
1a7ab6090777: Pull complete 
f846c9d928fd: Pull complete 
Digest: sha256:6505203f9adcc2e1c751555c750ab117b67775e05ca0ffefb6b4a69c9e504aee
Status: Downloaded newer image for nvidia/cuda:9.2-cudnn7-devel
```

Also pulling a random image like `buildah pull fedora:28` also works...
Could you try the same command with buildah running as root?

I have a feeling that this image is attempting to create a device node which is not allowed in usernamespace.
@giuseppe WDYT?

I think this PR will fix it: https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/237

It is blocked on review though

@rhatdan 

That worked, as it downloads everything from scratch. It seems to be using a different blob cache since the first four blobs were re-downloaded. Where is the cache located, and how can I purge it?
```
$ sudo buildah pull docker.io/nvidia/cuda:9.2-cudnn7-devel
[sudo] password for xxxx: 
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:18d680d616571900d78ee1c8fff0310f2a2afe39c6ed0ba2651ff667af406c3e
 41.34 MiB / 41.34 MiB [===================================================] 35s
Copying blob sha256:0addb6fece630456e0ab187b0aa4304d0851ba60576e7f6f9042a97ee908a796
 851 B / 851 B [============================================================] 0s
Copying blob sha256:78e58219b215b359fe002f0ca1f416617b75ca9b36cb274c98d7a5f808711179
 620 B / 620 B [============================================================] 0s
Copying blob sha256:eb6959a66df2ea26a26452ba11f84fb64bb45af90204374862ec71ee59f795e7
 169 B / 169 B [============================================================] 0s
Copying blob sha256:c7f8a5420911af2d94a05755e569e58da2e33de4b199fbc4a22771cd3f72023f
 6.65 MiB / 6.65 MiB [======================================================] 6s
Copying blob sha256:f20e55ec643a8ef56c529bdbc0ce5486c95735b52536330913d7c52003759e74
 386.20 KiB / 386.20 KiB [==================================================] 1s
Copying blob sha256:964bd7cf2ca319d92423a69eb3411a1480fd35287634cc6cc9edf6b2e53e85f9
 181 B / 181 B [============================================================] 0s
Copying blob sha256:db218380e512e0e48f2c3bf3fc9e9accb63778483193b4e41b6d98a5a3febff3
 466.85 MiB / 466.85 MiB [===============================================] 3m29s
Copying blob sha256:1a7ab6090777cfb12f1e111661f13d9cef97479b536e3646017913366b79bf96
 652.26 MiB / 652.26 MiB [===============================================] 6m47s
Copying blob sha256:f846c9d928fd5f5ef3370df6e6f9068764566dabad8ba1e22770840bb9205d06
 358.83 MiB / 358.83 MiB [===============================================] 7m27s
Copying config sha256:7affc4c2ce6186d6caab0880673a2c29f909b4d120aabaa4c6b6b253d8176b64

Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
7affc4c2ce6186d6caab0880673a2c29f909b4d120aabaa4c6b6b253d8176b64
```

Retrying `buildah pull docker.io/nvidia/cuda:9.2-cudnn7-devel` immediately afterwards fails in the exact same way.

Also curiously (or not), this works - note `cuda:8.0` rather than `cuda:9.2`:
```
$ buildah rmi 13a0e139712b && buildah pull docker.io/nvidia/cuda:8.0-cudnn7-devel
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:18d680d616571900d78ee1c8fff0310f2a2afe39c6ed0ba2651ff667af406c3e
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:0addb6fece630456e0ab187b0aa4304d0851ba60576e7f6f9042a97ee908a796
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:78e58219b215b359fe002f0ca1f416617b75ca9b36cb274c98d7a5f808711179
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:eb6959a66df2ea26a26452ba11f84fb64bb45af90204374862ec71ee59f795e7
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:c6aa9245dd492d46dbc70c6038026b349f9d5412d2543402690a46ed0bd1e56e
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:f0233a859d9b59fc71d3adcaec0413bcd2cc5749a6ff42a4896fd0f7e80e9106
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:b4c3e8ab5f0192fc58d1dcc2b8a2ab613fc6048bb3f5115f5c32694a112a9c1a
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:6a69efc66f061b663e5c6d6633df78eb331cedfce61f8e43093c1a56d7cfe6a2
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:63fcf98bb5a36f6450521e6f09576599571ac616b25b017fe34f3be4fb7edc34
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:e49cd3d0baa155ad2683676d1bd5569db0860f516128727fc9bcec9958bc044b
Copying config sha256:13a0e139712be440e8a5c745cd3e1caaa9c79ce74a12de77d8c15a9d76bff3c9
 10.12 KiB / 10.12 KiB [====================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
13a0e139712be440e8a5c745cd3e1caaa9c79ce74a12de77d8c15a9d76bff3c9
```
It seems this specific layer:
```
Copying blob sha256:c7f8a5420911af2d94a05755e569e58da2e33de4b199fbc4a22771cd3f72023f
 6.65 MiB / 6.65 MiB [======================================================] 7s
```
Is not cached correctly. Is there any way to manually remove this cached item?

@giuseppe How would I go about applying that PR?

...and now I've trashed my installation as `buildah from docker.io/nvidia/cuda:8.0-cudnn7-devel` produces an error:

```
error creating container: error creating read-write layer with ID "badb74c99b852f50637bf1111baa492564519e956d12a52dc29bc4c06025335e": no such file or directory
ERRO[0000] exit status 1                                
```

I would guess that my previous `sudo buildah` has set some critical files or folders as `root`, but which ones? Trying with debug doesn't help me understand:

```
$ buildah --debug from docker.io/nvidia/cuda:8.0-cudnn7-devel
DEBU[0000] running [buildah-in-a-user-namespace --debug from docker.io/nvidia/cuda:8.0-cudnn7-devel] with environment [**snipped** _=/usr/local/bin/buildah _BUILDAH_STARTED_IN_USERNS=1 BUILDAH_ISOLATION=rootless], UID map [{HostID:1000 ContainerID:0 Size:1} {HostID:100000 ContainerID:1 Size:65536}], and GID map [{HostID:1000 ContainerID:0 Size:1} {HostID:100000 ContainerID:1 Size:65536}] 
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "vfs"   
DEBU[0000] error parsing image name "docker.io/nvidia/cuda:8.0-cudnn7-devel" as given, trying with transport "docker://": Invalid image name "docker.io/nvidia/cuda:8.0-cudnn7-devel", unknown transport "docker.io/nvidia/cuda" 
DEBU[0000] parsed reference into "[vfs@/home/xxxx/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/1000]docker.io/nvidia/cuda:8.0-cudnn7-devel" 
DEBU[0000] parsed reference into "[vfs@/home/xxxx/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/1000]docker.io/nvidia/cuda:8.0-cudnn7-devel" 
ERRO[0000] error creating container: error creating read-write layer with ID "6dbbcce0e8ac6b1ff1fc4de97f2ecdefb8df109e9de9b8dc751f86844a964072": no such file or directory 
ERRO[0000] exit status 1                                
```

I finally seem to have got it back into a working state. The successful commands were:

```
sudo rm -rf ~/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs/dir/*
rm -rf ~/.local/share/containers/storage
```

Just the first on its own wasn't enough - nuking the whole tree was necessary. I'll not close this as I think there is a real bug regarding `error creating container: error creating read-write layer`, although if you disagree I'll not complain!
@KenYN to try out my PR, you would need to copy the modified files under `vendor/github.com/containers/storage` and rebuild buildah.
